name: Reinforcement-Learning Agent with Microbiota-Modulated Reward Shaping
description: Tabular Q-learning agent whose reward signal is shaped by a dynamic gut microbiota state. The microbiota produces SCFAs that stimulate GLP-1 secretion, which in turn attenuates the perceived reward of eating when the agent is sated. Diet diversity maintains microbiota health. This models the microbiota→SCFA→GLP-1 cascade from Nature Signal Transduction (2022) and MDPI Nutrients (2023), with Q-learning providing the adaptive decision-making layer. The key distinction from other formulations is that learning itself is modulated by gut state — the agent's reward landscape shifts based on its microbiome history.

variables.name: q_table
variables.description: Action-value function Q(s,a) mapping discrete states to expected cumulative reward for each action. Initialized to zeros.

variables.name: microbiota_health
variables.description: Index of gut microbiome diversity/health, slowly adapts to diet diversity

variables.name: scfa_level
variables.description: Short-chain fatty acid level produced by gut microbiota upon eating

variables.name: glp1_level
variables.description: Plasma GLP-1 driven by SCFA stimulation of L-cells and direct nutrient sensing

variables.name: energy_reserve
variables.description: Internal energy level depleted each tick and replenished by eating

variables.name: diet_diversity
variables.description: Rolling exponential average of recent eating activity, representing diet variety

variables.name: shaped_reward
variables.description: Internal reward signal perceived by the agent, modulated by GLP-1 satiety and energy state

variables.name: previous_state
variables.description: Discretized state from previous tick for Q-learning update

variables.name: previous_action
variables.description: Action taken on the previous tick

variables.name: ate_last_tick
variables.description: Binary indicator of whether the agent ate last tick

parameters.name: scfa_production_rate
parameters.source: Nature Signal Transduction (2022): SCFA production proportional to microbiota health

parameters.name: scfa_glp1_coupling
parameters.source: MDPI Nutrients (2023): SCFAs stimulate L-cell GLP-1 secretion

parameters.name: glp1_decay_rate
parameters.source: Yoo & Park (2021): DPP-4 mediated GLP-1 clearance

parameters.name: satiety_reward_shaping_coeff
parameters.source: PMC11483575 (2024): GLP-1 reduces hedonic value of food

parameters.name: energy_depletion_penalty
parameters.source: Starvation penalty when energy critically low

parameters.name: low_energy_threshold
parameters.source: Below this threshold, starvation penalty activates

rules.description: Microbiota health adapts slowly toward current diet diversity
rules.pseudocode:
M = M + mu_adapt * (D - M)
M = max(0.0, min(1.0, M))

rules.description: Diet diversity: rolling exponential average of eating activity
SCFA production: depends on microbiota health and eating, decays metabolically
rules.pseudocode:
SCFA = (1.0 - k_SCFA_decay) * SCFA + k_SCFA * M * ate_last_tick
SCFA = max(SCFA, 0.0)

rules.description: GLP-1 from SCFA cascade: the microbiota → EEC → peptide pathway
rules.pseudocode:
H_GLP1 = (1.0 - k_GLP1_decay) * H_GLP1 + k_GLP1_SCFA * SCFA * ate_last_tick
H_GLP1 = max(H_GLP1, 0.0)

rules.description: Energy dynamics: depleted by basal cost, replenished by eating
rules.pseudocode:
E = E - E_cost + ate_last_tick * 1.0
E = max(E, 0.0)

rules.description: Shaped reward: environment reward attenuated by GLP-1 satiety, plus energy depletion penalty
rules.pseudocode:
satiety_factor = 1.0 - phi_sat * H_GLP1
satiety_factor = max(satiety_factor, 0.0)
energy_penalty = phi_energy * max(0.0, E_threshold - E)
R_shaped = R_env * satiety_factor + energy_penalty

s_curr = discretize_state(H_GLP1, food_nearby, M)
max_Q_next = max(Q.get((s_curr, a), 0.0) for a in all_actions)
old_Q = Q.get((s_prev, a_prev), 0.0)
Q[(s_prev, a_prev)] = old_Q + alpha_lr * (R_shaped + gamma_disc * max_Q_next - old_Q)

rules.description: State discretization: maps continuous variables to 12-state discrete space
rules.pseudocode:
# Hunger bin: high GLP-1 means low hunger
h_bin = int(min(2, max(0, floor(3.0 * (1.0 - min(H_GLP1, 1.0))))))
# Food nearby: 1 if any food at current position or adjacent cells, else 0
f_near = 1 if any food within Manhattan distance 1 else 0
# Microbiota bin: 1 if healthy, 0 if unhealthy
m_bin = 1 if M > 0.5 else 0
s = (h_bin, f_near, m_bin)

decision_logic.description: Tabular Q-learning with epsilon-greedy action selection. The agent updates its internal gut-microbiota cascade, computes shaped reward from the previous action, updates Q-values, discretizes the current state, and selects an action. The eat action is constrained to only be chosen when food is at the current position.
pseudocode: apply_rules(R2, R1, R3, R4, R5)  # update D, M, SCFA, H_GLP1, E
# 2. Compute shaped reward for previous action
R_env = reward  # reward from environment for previous action
# 4. Q-learning update for previous (state, action) pair
food_here = any(f for f in perception.resources.food if f.x == perception.x and f.y == perception.y)
action = argmax(Q.get((s_curr, a), 0.0) for a in available_actions)
# 6. Store state and action for next tick's Q-update

perception_to_variables.ate_last_tick: 1 if last action was 'eat' and reward > 0, else 0
perception_to_variables.R_env: reward parameter from update(action, reward, new_perception)
perception_to_variables.food_nearby: any food with Manhattan distance <= 1 from (perception.x, perception.y)

env_mapping.reward_source: eat action → ConsumeEffect reward of 1.0, then shaped via R6 with GLP-1 satiety attenuation and energy penalty

expected_behaviors.description: Eating when GLP-1 is high yields diminished shaped reward, discouraging overeating
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: agent eats twice in a row → assert R_shaped for second eat is lower than first due to elevated H_GLP1

expected_behaviors.description: Energy depletion drives urgent foraging behavior via energy penalty in shaped reward
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: set E below E_threshold → assert R_shaped includes negative penalty → agent Q-values favor eating/movement toward food

expected_behaviors.description: Microbiota health improves with regular eating, amplifying SCFA→GLP-1 cascade
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: agent eats regularly for 50 ticks → assert M increases toward D → SCFA production per meal increases

expected_behaviors.description: Agent learns to avoid eating when already sated (Q-value for eat decreases in low-hunger state)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: after 200 steps of learning → in state (h_bin=0, f_near=1, m_bin=1), assert Q(s, eat) < Q(s, stay)

expected_behaviors.description: Healthy microbiota state (m_bin=1) yields different Q-values than unhealthy (m_bin=0)
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: after training → assert Q((2,1,1), eat) != Q((2,1,0), eat) — microbiota modulates learned policy

expected_behaviors.description: Starvation-level energy triggers proactive food-seeking even if GLP-1 hasn't fully cleared
expected_behaviors.test_pseudocode: E = 0.5, H_GLP1 = 0.3 → energy_penalty dominates → agent moves toward food

references: MDPI Nutrients (2023) — The Mechanism of the Gut-Brain Axis in Regulating Food Intake — Nutrients 15(17):3728 (PubMed 37686760)
Nature Signal Transduction and Targeted Therapy (2022) — Gut microbes regulate the feeding center: a new discovery of gut-brain axis
Frontiers in Cellular and Infection Microbiology (2023) — Dynamical alterations of brain function and gut microbiota in obesity
Yoo, J.Y. & Park, S. (2021) — Role of the gut-brain axis in energy and glucose metabolism — npj Science of Food / Nature
Pharmacology Research & Perspectives (2024) — The gut-brain axis in appetite, satiety, food intake, and eating disorders — PMC11483575
